sources.title: Marie Galante
sources.description: An early Spencer Tracy outing in this spy thriller set in Panama.

sources.title: 1934 - Marie Galante - Is she the world's Most Dangerous woman? or a victim of circumstance?
sources.description:
On the French coast, unlucky Marie Galante (Ketti Gallian) is abducted and forced to board an American cargo ship bound for the Panama Canal. When an escape attempt leaves Marie high and dry in the Yucatan, she takes work as a nightclub singer to earn her safe passage to the Canal region. But Marie faces bigger problems when she gets mixed up in a destructive plot against the U.S. Naval fleet, and so she accepts the kindly assistance of secret agent Dr. Crawbett (Spencer Tracy).

Colorized version: https://youtu.be/gfO3myHriaY

Director: Henry King
Writers: Reginald Berkeley, Jacques Deval, William Absalom Drake
Stars: Spencer Tracy, Ketti Gallian, Ned Sparks
Genres: Drama, Espionage, Conspiracy Thriller, Holiday Romance

sources.title: Marie Galante (1934) SPENCER TRACY
sources.description:
Stars: Ketti Gallian, Spencer Tracy, Helen Morgan
Director: Henry King

When a Parisian beauty is stranded in Panama, she sings at a nightclub to earn her fare home. Three men pursue her, one seeks to possess her, another to protect her, and the third wants to use her as a pawn in his diabolically scheme to blowup the Canal.

sources.title: Counter-espionage (1934) Spencer Tracy & Ketti Gallian - Thriller film directed by Henry King
sources.description:
Marie is kidnapped and taken aboard ship, then thrown off at Yucatan. She winds up singing in a café in the Panama Canal zone. There she gets involved in a plot to destroy the canal and runs into American intelligence officer Crawbett.

Original title: Marie Galante (1934)
Directed by Henry King

Cast:
Spencer Tracy - Dr. Crawbett, an American tropical disease specialist.
Ketti Gallian - Marie Galante
Ned Sparks - Plosser, curmudgeonly owner of the Pacific Gardens cabaret
Helen Morgan - Tapia, a singer at the Pacific Gardens
Sig Ruman - Brogard, owner of the Parisian Bazaar. He speaks with a German accent.
Leslie Fenton - General Saki Tenoki, retired from the Japanese Navy, dealer in curios
Arthur Byron - Panama Canal Zone Governor General Gerald Phillips
Robert Loraine - Ratcliff, a British agent
Frank Darien - Ellsworth, on Phillips's staff
Stepin Fetchit - waiter at the Pacific Gardens
Jay C. Flippen - Sailor in a Bar

B&W version: https://youtu.be/uBmG9WRzTCU

https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=UUMOycDFnpMeWzaITQSD1dWsOA

@CultCinemaClassics

metadata.Plot: Marie Galante, a pretty French deliverer of telegrams, is inadvertently abducted by a drunken sea captain bound for Central America. Escaping in Mexico, Marie travels down the coast trying to find a way back home to France. In the Panama Canal Zone, she finds work as a singer and piques the interest of several men: Tenoki, a Japanese merchant, Brogard, his German competitor, Ratcliff, a British diplomatic agent, and Dr. Crawbett, ostensibly a researcher into tropical diseases. In fact, one or more of these men is involved in espionage and a plot to sink the U.S. fleet as it passes through the canal. Naive Marie cannot believe anything nefarious is afoot, but the handsome Dr. Crawbett convinces her to help him uncover the saboteur. But Marie is not sure she can trust even Crawbett.
metadata.Writer: Reginald Berkeley, Jacques Deval, William Absalom Drake
metadata.Actors: Spencer Tracy, Ketti Gallian, Ned Sparks
